en.extract:
The West African pied hornbill (Lophoceros semifasciatus) is a bird of the hornbill family, a family of tropical near-passerine birds found in the Old World.
The West African pied hornbill is found in West Africa, from south Nigeria to Senegal and Gambia—primarily in secondary forest areas of the Guinean-Congolese forest, and is threatened by forest fragmentation. It was previously considered conspecific with the Congo pied hornbill, and was split in IOC 13.2.
It is a frugivore.


References


Further reading
Birds of The Gambia by Barlow, Wacher and Disley, ISBN 1-873403-32-1

The '''West African pied hornbill''' ('''''Lophoceros semifasciatus''''') is a bird of the [[hornbill]] family, a family of tropical [[near-passerine]] [[bird]]s found in the [[Old World]].<ref name=IOC>{{cite book|editor-last1=Gill |editor-first1=F |editor-first2=D |editor-last2=Donsker |editor-first3=P |editor-last3=Rasmussen |year=2023 |title=IOC World Bird List (v 13.2) |doi=10.14344/IOC.ML.13.2 |doi-broken-date=12 July 2025 |url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/}}</ref>

The West African pied hornbill is found in West Africa, from south [[Nigeria]] to [[Senegal]] and [[Gambia]]—primarily in [[secondary forest]] areas of the [[Guinea]]n-Congolese forest,{{citation needed|date=October 2023}} and is threatened by forest fragmentation.<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Mynott |first1=Holly Isabelle |last2=Lee |first2=David Charles |last3=Santillan |first3=Rhea Aranas |last4=Jürgen Schwarz |first4=Christian |last5=Tacud |first5=Benjamin |last6=Fernandez |first6=Arcel Dryden |last7=Kerhoas |first7=Daphne |date=2021-09-01 |title=Population assessment and habitat associations of the Visayan Hornbill ''Penelopides panini'' in Northwest Panay, Philippines |journal=Avian Research|volume=12 |issue=1 |page=67 |doi=10.1186/s40657-021-00303-3 |pmid=34868606 |pmc=8628822 |doi-access=free }}</ref> It was previously considered conspecific with the [[Congo pied hornbill]], and was split in IOC 13.2.<ref name=IOC/>

It is a frugivore.<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Chaves |first1=Patrícia P. |last2=Timóteo |first2=Sérgio |last3=Gomes |first3=Sara |last4=Rainho |first4=Ana |date=2022-03-25 |title=Response of avian and mammal seed dispersal networks to human-induced forest edges in a sub-humid tropical forest |journal=Journal of Tropical Ecology |volume=38 |issue=4 |pages=199–209 |doi=10.1017/s0266467422000062 |hdl=10451/52086 |s2cid=199414721 |issn=0266-4674|hdl-access=free }}</ref>
